personnel_information.types_of_certifications_held_by_employee:
Our staff maintains a range of environmental, safety, and construction-related certifications relevant to contractors pollution liability work. Key certifications include:

- OSHA 40-Hour HAZWOPER (29 CFR 1910.120) for all field technicians, project managers, and site supervisors, with current 8-hour annual refreshers documented and tracked through our safety management system.
- OSHA 30-Hour Construction Safety for all foremen, project managers, and senior field leads, focused on recognizing and controlling jobsite hazards, including those related to hazardous materials handling and confined spaces.
- Certified Hazardous Materials Manager (CHMM) – one senior project manager responsible for oversight of waste profiling, manifesting, and coordination with licensed transporters and disposal facilities.
- Certified Industrial Hygienist (CIH) – one part-time consulting hygienist under contract who supports air monitoring plans, personal exposure assessments, and development of site-specific health and safety plans for remediation and abatement projects.
- Asbestos Inspector and Asbestos Contractor/Supervisor licenses (state-specific for Missouri and Kansas) held by three staff members who perform pre-demolition surveys, clearance inspections, and oversee abatement subcontractors.
- Lead Risk Assessor and Lead Inspector certifications for residential and commercial lead-based paint projects, particularly for renovation and demolition jobs in older buildings where disturbance of painted surfaces is anticipated.
- NIOSH 582-equivalent training for fiber counting, used by our in-house microscopist to perform preliminary asbestos air clearance checks before sending confirmatory samples to an accredited laboratory.
- DOT Hazardous Materials Transportation training (49 CFR 172, Subpart H) for all drivers and shipping coordinators who handle or sign off on hazardous waste shipments, including container labeling, placarding, and emergency response awareness.
- Confined Space Entry and Confined Space Entry Supervisor training for staff who enter tanks, vaults, and utility corridors where potential atmospheric hazards exist, including procedures for monitoring oxygen levels and toxic vapors.
- First Aid/CPR/AED certifications for most field personnel and all supervisors, with at least one certified responder assigned to every active job site.
- Erosion and Sediment Control training (including one Certified Professional in Erosion and Sediment Control—CPESC—under contract) for projects involving large-scale soil disturbance, stormwater management, and control of sediment-laden runoff.
- Spill Prevention, Control, and Countermeasure (SPCC) training for key employees involved in bulk fuel handling, chemical storage management, and emergency spill response.

We maintain a central training matrix that documents each employee’s certifications, expiration dates, and required refreshers. This matrix is reviewed quarterly by our Safety & Compliance Coordinator to ensure that no one is assigned to work outside the scope of their current training and credentials. Copies of certificates and licenses are kept on file at our main office and digitally in our secure cloud-based compliance system.
